about_heading: What is an In-Hand Salary Calculator?
about_html:
ūüí° Effective zero-tax CTC is ~‚āĻ13.7 LPA ‚ÄĒ not ‚āĻ12 LPA. Since employer EPF and gratuity are excluded from your taxable income, salaried employees with CTC up to approximately ‚āĻ13.5‚Äď‚āĻ13.7 lakh pay zero income tax under the new regime for FY 2025-26.

An in-hand salary calculator helps you find out exactly how much money will be credited to your bank account each month after all statutory deductions. In India, your CTC (Cost to Company) and your actual take-home salary can differ significantly ‚ÄĒ sometimes by 20% to 30%.

This calculator accounts for all standard deductions applicable under Indian salary structure for FY {{YEAR}}-26, including Employee Provident Fund (EPF), Professional Tax, and income tax under both the new and old tax regimes.

Understanding your in-hand salary is essential for budgeting, planning loan EMIs, calculating monthly savings, and evaluating job offers accurately.
formula_html:
The basic formula for calculating monthly in-hand salary is:

Monthly In-Hand = (CTC ‚ąí Employer PF ‚ąí Gratuity) √∑ 12 ‚ąí Employee PF ‚ąí Professional Tax ‚ąí Income Tax

Step 1: Calculate Gross Monthly Salary

Gross monthly salary = Annual CTC √∑ 12. This is before any deductions.

Step 2: Calculate EPF Deduction

Employee's EPF contribution = 12% of Basic Salary. Basic salary is typically 40‚Äď50% of CTC. So on a ‚āĻ10 LPA CTC with ‚āĻ4 LPA basic, EPF = 12% √ó ‚āĻ33,333 = ‚āĻ4,000/month.

Step 3: Subtract Professional Tax

Professional tax varies by state. In Maharashtra: ‚āĻ200/month for salaries above ‚āĻ10,000. In some states it is nil.

Step 4: Calculate and Deduct Income Tax

Income tax is calculated on taxable income (gross salary minus applicable exemptions and deductions). The resulting annual tax is divided by 12 to get monthly TDS (Tax Deducted at Source).

1Annual CTC: ‚āĻ10,00,000
2Basic Salary (40% of CTC): ‚āĻ4,00,000/year = ‚āĻ33,333/month
3Employee EPF (12% of basic): ‚āĻ4,000/month
4Gross Monthly Salary: ‚āĻ10,00,000 √∑ 12 = ‚āĻ83,333
5Professional Tax (Maharashtra): ‚āĻ200/month
6Income Tax (new regime, standard deduction ‚āĻ75,000): approx. ‚āĻ5,417/month TDS
7Monthly In-Hand ‚Čą ‚āĻ83,333 ‚ąí ‚āĻ4,000 ‚ąí ‚āĻ200 ‚ąí ‚āĻ5,417 = ‚āĻ73,716

Note: Actual in-hand salary may vary based on your HRA, special allowances, and exact tax computation.

faqs.q: What is CTC and how does it differ from in-hand salary?
faqs.a: CTC (Cost to Company) is the total amount your employer spends on you annually, including your salary, PF contributions, gratuity, and other benefits. In-hand salary (or take-home pay) is the amount actually credited to your bank account after all deductions like employee PF (12% of basic), professional tax, and income tax.

faqs.q: How is PF calculated on salary?
faqs.a: Employee Provident Fund (EPF) is calculated at 12% of your basic salary plus DA. The employer also contributes 12%, but 8.33% goes to EPS and 3.67% to EPF. Only the employee's 12% is deducted from your in-hand salary.

faqs.q: Which tax regime is better ‚ÄĒ new or old?
faqs.a: The new tax regime (default from FY 2023-24) offers lower slab rates but no deductions. The old regime allows deductions like HRA, 80C (up to ‚āĻ1.5L), 80D, and home loan interest. Generally, if your total deductions exceed ‚āĻ3.75 lakh, the old regime is better.

faqs.q: What is professional tax and who pays it?
faqs.a: Professional tax is a state-level tax levied on salaried employees. The amount varies by state ‚ÄĒ in Maharashtra it is ‚āĻ200/month for salaries above ‚āĻ10,000. Some states like Delhi and Haryana do not levy professional tax.

faqs.q: Is HRA fully tax-exempt?
faqs.a: No. HRA exemption is the minimum of: actual HRA received, 50% of basic salary if metro (40% for non-metro), or actual rent paid minus 10% of basic salary. The remainder is taxable.

faqs.q: What is the in-hand salary for 10 LPA in {{YEAR}}?
faqs.a: For a ‚āĻ10 LPA CTC with 40% basic salary in a metro city under the new tax regime FY {{YEAR}}-26, the monthly in-hand salary is approximately ‚āĻ72,000‚Äď‚āĻ74,000 after EPF (‚āĻ4,000), professional tax (‚āĻ200), and income tax TDS (‚Čą‚āĻ4,500‚Äď‚āĻ5,000). The exact amount depends on your salary structure and HRA.

faqs.q: How much tax do I pay on 10 LPA under the new regime?
faqs.a: Under the new tax regime for FY {{YEAR}}-26, taxable income on ‚āĻ10 LPA (after ‚āĻ75,000 standard deduction) is approximately ‚āĻ7.97 lakh. Tax on this works out to roughly ‚āĻ57,000‚Äď‚āĻ62,000 annually including 4% cess, or about ‚āĻ4,750‚Äď‚āĻ5,200 per month as TDS.

faqs.q: Is 10 LPA a good salary in India?
faqs.a: Yes. ‚āĻ10 LPA places you in roughly the top 5‚Äď8% of Indian earners. In metro cities like Mumbai or Bengaluru the cost of living is higher, but in tier-2 cities a ‚āĻ10 LPA take-home of ~‚āĻ73,000/month provides a comfortable lifestyle with significant savings potential.

faqs.q: What is the difference between 10 LPA CTC and gross salary?
faqs.a: CTC includes employer contributions like EPF (12% of basic) and gratuity (4.81% of basic) that never reach your bank account. On ‚āĻ10 LPA, the employer's EPF and gratuity typically account for ‚āĻ1.1‚Äď‚āĻ1.3 lakh, so your actual gross salary is closer to ‚āĻ8.7‚Äď‚āĻ8.9 lakh per year.

about_heading: What is a Fuel Cost Per KM Calculator?
about_html:
A fuel cost per km calculator helps you understand the exact running cost of your vehicle for every kilometre you travel. This is one of the most practical tools for vehicle owners in India, where fuel prices vary daily and significantly impact monthly budgets.

Whether you own a petrol car, diesel car, CNG vehicle, or a two-wheeler, knowing your per-km fuel cost helps you budget travel expenses, compare the cost of driving versus public transport, and evaluate the true cost of a new vehicle purchase.

This calculator works for all fuel types and any vehicle class ‚ÄĒ from city hatchbacks to heavy trucks.
formula_heading: How is Fuel Cost Per KM Calculated?
formula_html:
The formula is straightforward:

Fuel Cost Per KM = Fuel Price (‚āĻ/litre) √∑ Mileage (km/litre)

To calculate monthly fuel cost:

Monthly Fuel Cost = Fuel Cost Per KM √ó Monthly Distance (km)

Factors that affect real-world mileage:

  • Driving conditions: City traffic vs. highway ‚ÄĒ highway gives 20‚Äď30% better mileage.
  • Vehicle age and maintenance: Worn spark plugs, dirty air filters, and low tyre pressure all reduce efficiency.
  • Air conditioning: Running AC increases fuel consumption by 8‚Äď15%.
  • Driving speed: Most petrol cars achieve peak efficiency between 60‚Äď80 km/h.
example_html:
ūüďč Example: Petrol car in Pune
1Current petrol price in Pune: ‚āĻ106.51/litre
2Vehicle mileage (Maruti Swift): 21 km/litre (ARAI certified)
3Real-world mileage (city driving ~75% of ARAI): ~15.75 km/litre
4Fuel cost per km = ‚āĻ106.51 √∑ 15.75 = ‚āĻ6.76 per km
5Monthly distance: 1,500 km ‚Üí Monthly cost = ‚āĻ10,143

Tip: Always use real-world mileage (70‚Äď80% of ARAI figures) for accurate estimates.

faqs.q: How is fuel cost per km calculated?
faqs.a: Fuel cost per km = Fuel price per litre √∑ Mileage (km per litre). For example, if petrol is ‚āĻ106/litre and your car gives 15 km/litre, the cost per km is ‚āĻ106 √∑ 15 = ‚āĻ7.07 per km.

faqs.q: What is a good mileage for a petrol car in India?
faqs.a: For a petrol hatchback or sedan, 15‚Äď20 km/litre is considered good mileage under city conditions. SUVs return 12‚Äď16 km/litre. Bikes and scooters generally offer 40‚Äď60 km/litre.

faqs.q: How do I find the current petrol or diesel price?
faqs.a: Fuel prices in India change daily and vary by city. Check via Indian Oil, BPCL, or HPCL websites, or SMS 'RSP' to 9224992249 (Indian Oil). The price shown at petrol pumps already includes all taxes and dealer margins.

faqs.q: How does driving style affect fuel cost per km?
faqs.a: Aggressive acceleration and hard braking can reduce mileage by 15‚Äď30%. Driving at 60‚Äď80 km/h gives optimal mileage. Properly inflated tyres, clean air filters, and regular servicing also improve fuel efficiency significantly.

about_heading: What is an EMI Calculator?
about_html:
An EMI (Equated Monthly Instalment) calculator helps you find the exact monthly payment required to repay a loan within a specific time period at a given interest rate. It is one of the most essential financial planning tools for anyone considering a home loan, car loan, personal loan, or education loan in India.

Before taking any loan, it is critical to calculate the EMI to ensure it fits within your monthly budget. Financial advisors generally recommend that your total EMI obligations should not exceed 40‚Äď50% of your monthly take-home salary.

This calculator also shows you the total interest you will pay over the life of the loan ‚ÄĒ a figure that is often surprisingly large and highlights the importance of choosing the right loan tenure.
formula_heading: EMI Formula and How It Works
formula_html:
The standard EMI formula used by all banks and financial institutions is:

EMI = P √ó r √ó (1 + r)^n √∑ ((1 + r)^n ‚ąí 1)

Where:

  • P = Principal loan amount (‚āĻ)
  • r = Monthly interest rate = Annual rate √∑ 12 √∑ 100
  • n = Loan tenure in months

Total Payment and Total Interest

Total Amount Paid = EMI √ó n
Total Interest Paid = Total Amount Paid ‚ąí Principal

Why Tenure Matters

On a ‚āĻ50 lakh home loan at 9% p.a., a 20-year tenure costs ‚āĻ49.8 lakh in interest versus ‚āĻ23.8 lakh for a 10-year tenure. Choosing a shorter tenure saves over ‚āĻ26 lakh in interest.
example_html:
ūüďč Example: Home Loan of ‚āĻ50 Lakhs at 9%
1Principal (P): ‚āĻ50,00,000
2Annual rate: 9% ‚Üí Monthly rate (r): 0.0075
3Tenure: 20 years = 240 months (n)
4EMI = 50,00,000 √ó 0.0075 √ó (1.0075)^240 √∑ ((1.0075)^240 ‚ąí 1)
5Monthly EMI ‚Čą ‚āĻ44,986
6Total paid = ‚āĻ44,986 √ó 240 = ‚āĻ10,79,664 ‚Üí Total interest ‚Čą ‚āĻ57.97 lakh

This shows why comparing loan tenures carefully before signing is so important.

faqs.q: What is an EMI?
faqs.a: EMI stands for Equated Monthly Instalment. It is the fixed amount you pay to the lender every month until the loan is fully repaid. Each EMI consists of two parts: the principal repayment and the interest charged on the outstanding balance.

faqs.q: What is the formula for calculating EMI?
faqs.a: EMI = P √ó r √ó (1 + r)^n √∑ ((1 + r)^n ‚ąí 1), where P is the principal, r is the monthly interest rate (annual rate √∑ 12 √∑ 100), and n is the loan tenure in months.

faqs.q: Does prepaying a loan reduce my EMI?
faqs.a: Prepayment reduces your outstanding principal, lowering total interest payable. Banks typically offer two options: reduce the tenure (keeping EMI the same) or reduce the EMI (keeping tenure the same). Reducing tenure saves more interest overall.

faqs.q: What is a good EMI to salary ratio?
faqs.a: Financial advisors recommend keeping total monthly EMIs below 40‚Äď50% of your net take-home salary. If your in-hand salary is ‚āĻ50,000, total EMIs should ideally not exceed ‚āĻ20,000‚Äď‚āĻ25,000.

faqs.q: Are there charges beyond the EMI?
faqs.a: Yes. Depending on your lender, you may also pay a processing fee (0.5‚Äď2% of loan amount), GST on that fee, prepayment charges, and late payment penalties if you miss an EMI.

about_heading: What is a SIP Calculator?
about_html:
ūüí° The real power of SIP is time, not amount. ‚āĻ5,000/month for 20 years at 12% grows to ‚āĻ49.9 lakh ‚ÄĒ but the same SIP for 30 years grows to ‚āĻ1.76 crore. Starting early matters far more than starting big.

A SIP (Systematic Investment Plan) calculator helps you estimate how much your regular monthly investment will grow over time. It uses the compound interest formula adjusted for monthly contributions ‚ÄĒ giving you a realistic picture of your wealth at any future date.

SIP calculators are used for goal-based planning ‚ÄĒ whether you're saving for a home down payment, children's education, or retirement corpus. Enter your monthly amount, duration, and expected return ‚ÄĒ the calculator shows your total corpus instantly.

For equity mutual funds, a 12% annual return is commonly used as a conservative long-term estimate based on historical Nifty 50 performance. For debt funds, 6‚Äď8% is more appropriate.
formula_html:
FV = P √ó [((1 + r)‚ĀŅ ‚ąí 1) / r] √ó (1 + r)

Where FV = Future Value (corpus), P = Monthly SIP amount, r = Monthly interest rate (annual rate √∑ 12), n = Total number of months.

Example ‚ÄĒ ‚āĻ5,000/month for 10 years at 12%

r = 12% √∑ 12 = 1% per month = 0.01. n = 10 √ó 12 = 120 months. FV = 5000 √ó [((1.01)¬Ļ¬≤‚Āį ‚ąí 1) / 0.01] √ó 1.01 = approximately ‚āĻ11.6 lakh.

Wealth Ratio

Wealth ratio = Total corpus √∑ Total invested. A ratio of 2√ó means your money doubled. At 12% for 15 years, wealth ratio is typically 2.5‚Äď3√ó. At 20 years it crosses 4√ó.

faqs.q: How is SIP return calculated?
faqs.a: SIP returns use the future value of annuity formula: FV = P √ó [((1+r)‚ĀŅ ‚ąí 1) / r] √ó (1+r), where P is monthly investment, r is monthly rate (annual √∑ 12), and n is total months. This accounts for compounding on each monthly installment.

faqs.q: What is a good expected return for SIP?
faqs.a: For equity mutual funds (large cap), 10‚Äď12% is a conservative long-term estimate based on Nifty 50 historical returns. Mid/small cap funds may yield 13‚Äď15% over long periods but with higher volatility. Debt funds typically return 6‚Äď8%.

faqs.q: Is SIP better than FD?
faqs.a: For long-term goals (5+ years), equity SIPs have historically outperformed FDs significantly. FDs return 6‚Äď7% while equity SIPs have averaged 11‚Äď13% over 10+ year periods. However SIPs carry market risk ‚ÄĒ FDs are capital protected.

faqs.q: What is wealth ratio in SIP?
faqs.a: Wealth ratio = Total maturity corpus √∑ Total amount invested. A ratio of 3√ó means for every ‚āĻ1 you invested, you got ‚āĻ3 back. At 12% annual return, a 15-year SIP typically achieves 2.8√ó wealth ratio, and a 20-year SIP achieves 4.2√ó.

faqs.q: Can I increase my SIP amount over time?
faqs.a: Yes ‚ÄĒ this is called a Step-Up SIP. For example, increasing your SIP by 10% every year dramatically improves your final corpus. A ‚āĻ5,000 SIP with 10% annual step-up for 20 years produces nearly double the corpus of a flat ‚āĻ5,000 SIP.

PPF (Public Provident Fund) is India's most popular long-term savings instrument ‚ÄĒ government-backed, completely tax-free (EEE status), and currently earning 7.1% interest per annum compounded annually.

The EEE (Exempt-Exempt-Exempt) tax treatment means: investment is deductible under 80C, interest earned is tax-free, and maturity amount is tax-free. For someone in the 30% bracket, the effective return is closer to 10.1%.

PPF has a 15-year lock-in but can be extended in 5-year blocks indefinitely. Partial withdrawals are allowed from year 7 onwards.

F = P √ó [((1+r)‚ĀŅ ‚ąí 1) / r] √ó (1+r)

Where F = Maturity amount, P = Annual investment, r = Annual interest rate (7.1%), n = Number of years.

Current PPF Rate

PPF interest rate for FY 2025-26 is 7.1% per annum, compounded annually. Interest is calculated on the lowest balance between 5th and last day of each month ‚ÄĒ so always invest before the 5th to earn interest for that month.

EEE Tax Benefit

For a 30% taxpayer investing ‚āĻ1.5L/year, the 80C deduction saves ‚āĻ46,800 in tax annually. Effective yield accounting for tax saved: approximately 10.1%.

faqs.a: The PPF interest rate for FY 2025-26 is 7.1% per annum, compounded annually. The rate is reviewed quarterly by the government but has remained at 7.1% since April 2020.

faqs.q: How much will PPF give after 15 years at max investment?
faqs.a: Investing ‚āĻ1.5 lakh/year for 15 years at 7.1% gives a maturity amount of approximately ‚āĻ40.68 lakh. Total invested is ‚āĻ22.5 lakh and interest earned is ‚āĻ18.18 lakh ‚ÄĒ all completely tax-free.

faqs.q: Can I extend PPF beyond 15 years?
faqs.a: Yes ‚ÄĒ PPF can be extended in 5-year blocks after the initial 15-year period. You can extend with or without contributions. Extending with contributions at 7.1% for 5 more years (20 years total) on ‚āĻ1.5L/year investment grows corpus to ‚āĻ66.58 lakh.

faqs.q: Is PPF better than FD?
faqs.a: For long-term savings, yes. PPF at 7.1% is tax-free, while FD interest is fully taxable. For a 30% taxpayer, a 7.1% PPF is equivalent to a 10.14% pre-tax FD ‚ÄĒ which no bank offers. PPF also carries zero default risk being government-backed.

faqs.q: When should I invest in PPF for maximum interest?
faqs.a: Always invest before the 5th of April (beginning of financial year) to earn interest for the full year. PPF interest is calculated on the minimum balance between the 5th and last day of each month ‚ÄĒ investing after the 5th means losing one month's interest.

about_heading: What is HRA Exemption?
about_html:
ūüí° HRA exemption is the lowest of 3 values. Many employees claim less HRA exemption than they're entitled to ‚ÄĒ use this calculator to find your exact tax-free amount.

HRA (House Rent Allowance) exemption under Section 10(13A) allows salaried employees to reduce taxable income by the HRA they receive ‚ÄĒ subject to certain limits. The exemption is only available under the old tax regime.

The exemption is the minimum of three amounts: (1) Actual HRA received, (2) 50% of basic salary for metro cities or 40% for non-metro, (3) Actual rent paid minus 10% of basic salary.

HRA exemption is only available under the old tax regime. Under the new regime, full HRA is taxable ‚ÄĒ making HRA a key reason some employees prefer the old regime.
formula_html:
HRA Exempt = Min of: (1) HRA received, (2) 50%/40% of basic, (3) Rent ‚ąí 10% of basic

Metro vs Non-Metro

Metro cities (Mumbai, Delhi, Kolkata, Chennai): 50% of basic salary. All other cities: 40% of basic salary. This is one of the few tax rules where city classification directly affects your tax bill.

Example ‚ÄĒ ‚āĻ50,000 basic, ‚āĻ25,000 HRA, ‚āĻ20,000 rent, Metro

(1) HRA received: ‚āĻ25,000. (2) 50% of basic: ‚āĻ25,000. (3) Rent ‚ąí 10% of basic: ‚āĻ20,000 ‚ąí ‚āĻ5,000 = ‚āĻ15,000. Exemption = Min(25,000, 25,000, 15,000) = ‚āĻ15,000/month.

faqs.q: How is HRA exemption calculated?
faqs.a: HRA exemption is the minimum of three amounts: (1) actual HRA received from employer, (2) 50% of basic salary for metro cities or 40% for non-metro, (3) actual rent paid minus 10% of basic salary. The lowest of these three is your tax-free HRA.

faqs.q: Is HRA available under new tax regime?
faqs.a: No. HRA exemption under Section 10(13A) is only available under the old tax regime. Under the new regime, full HRA received is included in taxable income. This is a key reason to compare both regimes ‚ÄĒ if your HRA exemption is large, old regime may save more tax.

faqs.q: What are metro cities for HRA?
faqs.a: Only four cities qualify as metro for HRA: Mumbai, Delhi, Kolkata, and Chennai. All other cities ‚ÄĒ including Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Pune, Ahmedabad ‚ÄĒ are classified as non-metro for HRA purposes, getting 40% of basic instead of 50%.

faqs.q: Can I claim HRA without rent receipts?
faqs.a: For rent up to ‚āĻ1 lakh/year (‚āĻ8,333/month), no receipts are required ‚ÄĒ just a declaration. For rent above ‚āĻ1 lakh/year, you must submit rent receipts and landlord's PAN to your employer for TDS computation.

faqs.q: What if my rent is more than HRA received?
faqs.a: You can only claim exemption up to the HRA amount received from your employer ‚ÄĒ paying more rent than HRA doesn't increase exemption beyond what's received. However, excess rent above 10% of basic is counted in calculation 3, so higher rent does help if HRA received is the binding constraint.

about_heading: What is Gratuity and When Are You Eligible?
about_html:
ūüí° Minimum 5 years of continuous service required. If you have 4 years and 8 months, you qualify ‚ÄĒ 6+ additional months rounds up to the next year.

Gratuity is a lump-sum payment made by an employer to an employee as a token of gratitude for their service. It is governed by the Payment of Gratuity Act, 1972 ‚ÄĒ a statutory right, not discretionary.

You are eligible if: (1) you have completed 5 years of continuous service, (2) your employer has 10 or more employees. Gratuity is payable on resignation, retirement, death, or disablement.

The tax exemption limit for gratuity is ‚āĻ20 lakh for private sector employees. Any amount above ‚āĻ20 lakh is taxable as salary income in the year of receipt.
formula_heading: Gratuity Formula ‚ÄĒ Payment of Gratuity Act
formula_html:
Gratuity = (Basic Salary √ó 15 √ó Years of Service) √∑ 26

Where: Basic Salary = last drawn basic + dearness allowance. 15 = half month's salary per year. 26 = working days in a month (statutory ‚ÄĒ not 30 or 31).

Rounding Rule

If additional months ‚Č• 6, round up to next full year. So 10 years 7 months = 11 years for calculation. 10 years 4 months = 10 years.

Example ‚ÄĒ ‚āĻ50,000 basic, 10 years

Gratuity = (50,000 √ó 15 √ó 10) √∑ 26 = ‚āĻ7,500,000 √∑ 26 = ‚āĻ2,88,462. Fully tax-free as it is under ‚āĻ20 lakh limit.
example_html:

10yrBasic ‚āĻ50K √ó 10 yrs ‚Üí Gratuity: ‚āĻ2,88,462
20yrBasic ‚āĻ80K √ó 20 yrs ‚Üí Gratuity: ‚āĻ9,23,077
30yrBasic ‚āĻ1L √ó 30 yrs ‚Üí Gratuity: ‚āĻ17,30,769 (tax-free)

faqs.q: What is the gratuity formula in India?
faqs.a: Gratuity = (Last Drawn Basic Salary √ó 15 √ó Years of Service) √∑ 26. The 15 represents half a month's salary per year of service, and 26 is the statutory number of working days in a month under the Payment of Gratuity Act, 1972.

faqs.q: Is gratuity taxable?
faqs.a: For private sector employees, gratuity up to ‚āĻ20 lakh is completely tax-free. Any amount above ‚āĻ20 lakh is added to your income and taxed at your applicable slab rate in the year of receipt. Government employees receive full tax exemption with no upper limit.

faqs.q: What is the minimum service for gratuity?
faqs.a: 5 years of continuous service is the minimum requirement. However, if you have completed 4 years and 6 months or more, it rounds up to 5 years ‚ÄĒ making you eligible. Less than 4 years 6 months does not qualify.

faqs.q: Is gratuity calculated on basic salary or CTC?
faqs.a: Gratuity is calculated on Basic Salary + Dearness Allowance only ‚ÄĒ not on CTC. HRA, special allowances, bonus, and other components are excluded. This is why your actual gratuity may be lower than online estimates that use total salary.

faqs.q: Can I get gratuity if I resign before 5 years?
faqs.a: No ‚ÄĒ resignation before completing 5 years (or 4 years 6 months) does not entitle you to gratuity under the Payment of Gratuity Act. However, some companies have their own gratuity policies that pay out earlier ‚ÄĒ check your employment contract.

ūüí° New regime is better for most people with low deductions. Old regime wins only if your total deductions are high. The break-even point at ‚āĻ12L income is roughly ‚āĻ3.5 lakh in deductions.

From FY 2023-24, the New Tax Regime became the default. You must actively opt into the Old Regime when filing your ITR if you want to claim deductions.

New Regime advantages: Lower slab rates, higher standard deduction (‚āĻ75,000), zero tax up to ‚āĻ12 lakh (after rebate). No paperwork for deductions.

Old Regime advantages: Allows deductions ‚ÄĒ 80C (‚āĻ1.5L), 80D (‚āĻ25K), HRA exemption, home loan interest (‚āĻ2L), NPS (‚āĻ50K). Worth it only if you actually invest and claim these.

Key rule: You cannot switch regimes mid-year if you have business income. Salaried employees can switch every year at time of ITR filing.

faqs.q: Which tax regime is better ‚ÄĒ new or old?
faqs.a: New regime is better if your total deductions (80C + 80D + HRA + others) are below ‚āĻ3‚Äď3.5 lakh. Old regime is better if you actively invest and claim high deductions. Use this calculator to compare with your exact numbers ‚ÄĒ the answer varies by income level and deduction amount.

faqs.q: Is income up to ‚āĻ12 lakh tax-free in the new regime?
faqs.a: Yes ‚ÄĒ for FY 2025-26, the Section 87A rebate in the new regime means zero tax if your taxable income (after ‚āĻ75,000 standard deduction) is up to ‚āĻ12 lakh. So gross salary up to ‚āĻ12.75 lakh has zero tax liability under the new regime.

faqs.q: Can I switch between new and old regime every year?
faqs.a: Salaried employees with no business income can switch regimes every year when filing their ITR. If you have business or professional income, you can switch only once. The new regime is the default ‚ÄĒ you must explicitly opt for the old regime.

faqs.q: What deductions are allowed in the new regime?
faqs.a: The new regime allows very few deductions: ‚āĻ75,000 standard deduction, NPS employer contribution (80CCD(2)), Agniveer corpus, and a few others. It does NOT allow 80C, 80D, HRA exemption, home loan interest deduction, or most other common deductions.

faqs.q: What is the surcharge on income above ‚āĻ50 lakh?
faqs.a: Surcharge applies at 10% for income ‚āĻ50L‚Äď‚āĻ1Cr, 15% for ‚āĻ1‚Äď2Cr, 25% for ‚āĻ2‚Äď5Cr, and 37% above ‚āĻ5Cr (25% in new regime). Surcharge is applied on the tax amount, and then 4% health and education cess is added on top.
